key_insights.insight: Our brains are constantly rewiring themselves based on our actions and thoughts, a process known as neuroplasticity.
key_insights.quote: Literally, for every one of us listening, everyone listening to us right now, at the end of this conversation, their brain will be wired differently than when it started.

key_insights.insight: The brain's primary function is to ensure safety, followed by happiness, with success being a secondary function.
key_insights.quote: The primary function of the brain is to make you safe. Okay. And then the secondary function that we push as humans to, that brain to do is to invent iPhones and create podcasts and have amazing things, right? That's a secondary function. But believe it or not, before that secondary function, your brain is supposed to make you happy because happy is the ultimate form for you to perform in life.

key_insights.insight: Repeated actions, whether positive or negative, strengthen neural pathways, making those actions easier to perform in the future.
key_insights.quote: If you perform a certain function, your brain starts to build networks that make that function easier to perform in the future.

key_insights.insight: Gratitude trains the brain to focus on the positive aspects of life, rewiring it to notice and appreciate good things.
key_insights.quote: It was training your brain to look for what's right. Your brain, every night that you did it was like, okay, it seems he's going to be asking to call his mom a lot more often. It seems he's going to be asking for good things a lot more often. I might as well observe them. I might as well find them.

key_insights.insight: Rewiring the brain takes time and consistent effort, with initial recognition of new wiring taking around 21 days and replacement of old wiring taking potentially 21 months.
key_insights.quote: It will take 21 days, let's say, for your brain to recognize I need different wiring. And it will take maybe 21 months for your brain to say, and I don't need the old wiring anymore.

key_insights.insight: Even physical abilities, like athleticism, are significantly influenced by the brain's wiring, which determines motivation, perseverance, and execution.
key_insights.quote: Believe it or not, the research will tell you that a big part of being athletic is wiring of your brain, not your muscles. For your brain to be able to say, I will go even if I feel a little tired. I will go even if I feel a little busy. I will go and I will do the right exercises, even if the last push is a little painful.

key_insights.insight: Spending time in silence and reflection is a form of meditation that fosters wisdom and rewires the brain.
key_insights.quote: And the reality is, is because he's in constant reflection and meditation. He's sitting out there and he's spending hours and days in reflection and meditation, right?

actionable_advice.advice: Counteract every negative thought with at least one (ideally nine) positive ones.
actionable_advice.how_to: When a negative thought arises, consciously list nine positive aspects or observations related to the same situation.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice gratitude consistently by keeping a gratitude journal or reflecting on positive things.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate a few minutes each day to write down things you are grateful for, focusing on specific details and experiences.

actionable_advice.advice: Utilize commuting time for positive mental exercises, like gratitude or listening to uplifting content.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of cursing your commute, use that time to practice gratitude, listen to music or an educational podcast.

memorable_stories: Story of a friend's brain tumor being removed and the brain regrowing to fill the hole.
Anecdote about a photographer who spends months in silence outside indigenous tribes' villages to earn their trust, highlighting the wisdom gained through reflection.
contrarian_claims: The primary function of the brain is not success, but safety and happiness.
Being athletic is more about the brain's wiring than the muscles.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 95 - A Simple Hack To Achieve Maximum Happiness: Mo Gowdat
